decision: Use --thinking-mode CLI flag in ta-agent-ollama args instead of extra TOML field
rationale: AgentFrameworkManifest does not have an 'extra' or 'metadata' map field. Adding one would require a schema change to a shared crate. Passing --thinking-mode true/false directly in the profile's args list keeps all configuration in args, is parsed by clap's Option<bool>, and requires no struct changes.
alternatives: Add HashMap<String, toml::Value> extra field to AgentFrameworkManifest
Store as separate profile-level fields requiring new serde deserialization logic

decision: Inline bundled Qwen3.5 profile TOML as string constants in agent.rs instead of include_str!
rationale: include_str! with a relative path from apps/ta-cli/src/commands/agent.rs to the agents/ directory at the workspace root would be fragile (deep relative path, breaks if file layout changes). Inline string constants are compile-time checked and self-contained. The agents/ TOML files are still created for file-level discovery, but the install command uses the constants.
alternatives: Use include_str!("../../../../agents/qwen3.5-4b.toml")
Read from disk at runtime and bundle in the binary via build.rs

decision: Place resolve_model_auto as a standalone async fn rather than on OllamaClient
rationale: resolve_model_auto needs to be called before the final OllamaClient is constructed (since the model name is part of client init). Making it a standalone fn that takes base_url as &str keeps the responsibility boundary clean: client.rs is for HTTP mechanics, main.rs handles argument resolution logic.
alternatives: Add resolve_auto() method to OllamaClient
Move resolution into Args parsing

decision: Read plan phase status from staging workspace BEFORE auto_clean in draft.rs
rationale: The original code read from target_dir after auto_clean could delete the staging dir, causing a false-positive 'still pending' warning even when the agent correctly marked the phase done. The fix reads from goal.workspace_path (staging) before auto_clean runs, then falls back to target_dir only for the validation step if staging status is non-Done.
alternatives: Disable auto_clean before the check
Only read from target_dir after apply has copied files

decision: file_filters is Vec<String> instead of Option<String> for RenderContext and DraftCommands::View
rationale: Vec<String> enables multiple --file patterns in one command and is a natural fit for clap's repeatable args. Option<String> would require separate handling of the multi-pattern case. All four output adapters use the same matches_file_filters() helper from mod.rs.
alternatives: Option<String> with separate --file-pattern repeat flag
comma-separated string

decision: deny_artifact reads stdin interactively rather than launching an external agent subprocess
rationale: The spec says show stored rationale rather than launching an agent. Interactive stdin is simpler, always available, and correct for the v0.14.9.2 scope. Full agent interrogation is a future enhancement.
alternatives: Launch reviewer agent subprocess
Print rationale only without interaction

decision: ShellContext state updated via TailStarted message and view command string detection
rationale: The shell dispatches most commands via the daemon async API with no synchronous return. Monitoring TailStarted and intercepting 'view <id>' / 'ta draft view <id>' strings before dispatch is minimal-invasive and does not require changes to the daemon protocol.
alternatives: Add context-tracking channel messages from daemon
Intercept all CommandResponse messages

decision: Use 'workflow-<uuid>.json' filename prefix to distinguish WorkflowSession from TaSession in .ta/sessions/
rationale: Both TaSession and WorkflowSession are stored in the same directory. TaSession uses '<uuid>.json'. A prefix prevents collision and makes 'list all workflow sessions' a simple filename filter.
alternatives: Separate subdirectory (.ta/sessions/workflows/)
Different file extension (.workflow.json)

rationale: ta-session crate does not have an anyhow dependency. Using String for the error type avoids adding a new dependency to a foundational crate for a minor ergonomic benefit.
alternatives: Add anyhow to ta-session
Use a custom error enum

decision: run_session() spawns 'ta run --headless' as a subprocess rather than calling run::execute() directly
rationale: Governed workflow execution needs process isolation — each plan item runs in its own agent process with its own environment. Calling execute() directly would share state and make cleanup harder. This also matches the existing governed_workflow.rs pattern.
alternatives: Call run::execute() directly in-process
Use tokio::spawn with a shared executor

decision: AwaitHuman gate is implemented inline in run_session() rather than as a separate workflow step type
rationale: The gate needs to block the CLI process and interact with the terminal. A separate step type would require a channel-based pause/resume mechanism that doesn't exist yet. Inline prompt matches the v0.14.11 scope; can be refactored to a proper step type in v0.14.12.
alternatives: Implement as a workflow step type with mpsc channel
Write pause state and poll on resume

decision: from_brief() uses deterministic markdown parsing rather than calling an LLM planner agent
rationale: The PLAN.md item specified a 'stub planner agent'. A deterministic parser is testable, reproducible, and doesn't require a running daemon. LLM-enhanced planning can be added later without breaking the interface.
alternatives: Call configured LLM agent via ta run --headless
Parse JSON output from a planner binary

decision: Use text placeholder files for VisualDiffRenderer instead of actual image processing
rationale: The workspace has no image processing dependencies. Adding the `image` crate for pixel-level diffing would increase build time and binary size for a feature that can be incrementally improved. The placeholder approach satisfies the interface contract — the reviewer gets the diff file path. Full image processing can be added in a future phase.
alternatives: Add the `image` crate dependency for pixel-level diffing
Use external CLI tools (convert, ffmpeg) via spawn_with_timeout

decision: Name the WorkflowConfig field `draft` of type `DraftReviewConfig` rather than using DraftConfig directly
rationale: Grouping draft-review settings under a `[draft]` table in workflow.toml is more extensible — future draft-phase settings can be added without cluttering the top-level WorkflowConfig. The name `DraftReviewConfig` avoids confusion with `DraftPackage` and `DiffConfig` which are already in scope.
alternatives: Add asset_diff directly as a top-level field on WorkflowConfig
Use DraftConfig as the spec suggested (risks naming confusion)
